message:
chore(beads): close bd-j0fz (label attention case-insensitive — already done)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -270,7 +270,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-hzhd.1","title":"Use relative dates in test fixtures to avoid staleness-dependent failures","description":"Many test fixtures use absolute dates like ts('2025-01-15T10:00:00Z') or '2026-03-08T18:00:00Z'. These dates interact with staleness calculations (14-day warning, 30-day critical, 90-day cap) in triage.rs and alerts.rs.

As time passes, what was once a 'fresh' test issue becomes 'stale', potentially changing test results. Currently the tests pass because the staleness normalization uses relative time, but some assertion values may drift.

AFFECTED FILES (sample):
- src/model.rs lines 568-674 (8+ test fixtures with 2025 dates)
- src/analysis/triage.rs test fixtures
- src/export_sqlite.rs lines 1136-1201 (2026 dates)
- src/analysis/causal.rs test fixtures

APPROACH:
- Replace absolute timestamps with chrono::Utc::now() - Duration offsets
- Use helper functions like 'days_ago(n)' for clarity
- Ensure staleness-sensitive tests use dates relative to 'now'
- Keep conformance fixture dates absolute (they match Go reference output)

RISK: Changing dates in test fixtures may require updating many assertion values. Should be done carefully with snapshot updates.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":90,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:18:54.554686602Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:18:54.553492372Z","close_reason":"Added days_ago() helper in model.rs. Analysis showed existing absolute dates are NOT causing failures because staleness is computed relative to Utc::now(). The helper enables better practice for future tests. (commit 14a5319)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.1","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:25.017690744Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-hzhd.2","title":"Isolate history mode tests from real git repo state","description":"The TUI history mode tests (snap_history_git_mode, e2e_journey_history_deep_dive) read from the actual git history of the bvr repository. This means:

1. The commit count changes with every new commit (now redacted via redact_git_commit_count)
2. Commit SHAs in snapshots reference real commits that may be rebased/squashed
3. The test depends on running from within the bvr git repo

CURRENT MITIGATION:
- redact_git_commit_count() (added commit 9f8f265) masks the volatile commit count
- Snapshots capture the current rendering without asserting specific commit SHAs

IDEAL FIX:
- Create a temporary git repo in the test with known commits
- Set BvrApp.repo_root to the temp repo
- This makes the test fully deterministic and independent of the real repo

COMPLEXITY: Medium — requires understanding how the TUI discovers and reads git history, and setting up a realistic-enough temp repo with commits that reference test issue IDs.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":60,"created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:55:50.683851573Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:55:50.683106944Z","close_reason":"Expanded redact_git_volatile() to mask SHAs, dates, and commit counts in history snapshots (commit 558bba1). Full temp-repo isolation deferred as low-priority since redaction provides sufficient stability.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["stability","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:08:39.957070215Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-hzhd.2","depends_on_id":"bd-hzhd.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:09:02.151189447Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
